Package com.bonitasoft.engine.bpm.delegation

Interface DelegatedTask

All Superinterfaces:
ActivityInstance, BaseElement, BaseRestElement, BonitaObject, DescriptionElement, FlowNodeInstance, HumanTaskInstance, NamedElement, Serializable, TaskInstance
All Known Implementing Classes:
DelegatedTaskImpl
public interface DelegatedTask extends HumanTaskInstance, BaseRestElement
Read-only view of a HumanTaskInstance that the current user is allowed to act on through an active DelegationRule. Returned by DelegationAPI.searchDelegatedTasks(SearchOptions).

The task is still assigned to the delegator — delegation grants the delegate visibility and execution rights but does not reassign ownership. The audit trail is preserved via the engine's executedBy / executedBySubstitute fields.

Extends HumanTaskInstance so a future unified task-list API can return a single SearchResult<HumanTaskInstance> where delegated entries are distinguished via instanceof DelegatedTask and carry the additional delegator / delegate / delegation-window context exposed here. Also extends BaseRestElement so the id field is serialized as a JSON string (JavaScript precision).

JSON wire format

Long-typed inherited ids are re-declared with ToStringSerializer so the JSON output stays safe against Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (real 19-digit entity ids can exceed it). Delegation-window timestamps (delegationStart, delegationEnd) are serialized as numeric int64 epoch ms - 13-digit timestamps never exceed MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. Several inherited keys are renamed via @JsonProperty to align with the legacy HumanTaskItem REST shape used by the Bonita Portal — modernised to camelCase where the legacy form was snake_case:
  • processDefinitionId → processId
  • parentProcessInstanceId → parentCaseId
  • rootContainerId → rootCaseId
  • expectedEndDate → dueDate
  • claimedDate → assignedDate (legacy assigned_date modernised)
stateCategory and parentContainerId are hidden with @JsonIgnore — neither is exposed by HumanTaskItem, and parentContainerId duplicates parentCaseId in the common top-level-task case (it differs only for nested sub-tasks where it points at the parent activity, not the parent process instance).

  • Method Details

    • getRootProcessDeploymentInfo

      ProcessDeploymentInfo getRootProcessDeploymentInfo()
      Returns the deployment info of the root process — i.e. the top-level process whose deployment was started, even when the task lives inside a subprocess. Resolved from rootContainerId so that subprocess tasks are matched against the rule's process whitelist.

      Surfacing the full ProcessDeploymentInfo (rather than just the id) lets the frontend render the root process display name, version and icon without a second round-trip.

    • getDelegator

      User getDelegator()
      Returns the delegator (the user whose task this is).

    • getDelegate

      User getDelegate()
      Returns the delegate (the user the task is currently surfaced to through delegation).

    • getDelegationStart

      long getDelegationStart()
      Returns the start of the delegation period (epoch millis), inclusive. Same value as DelegationRule.startDate; copied here so the front-end can render the delegation context without a second round-trip.

    • getDelegationEnd

      long getDelegationEnd()
      Returns the end of the delegation period (epoch millis), inclusive.

    • getActorId

      long getActorId()
      Specified by:
      getActorId in interface HumanTaskInstance

    • getProcessDefinitionId

      long getProcessDefinitionId()
      Description copied from interface: FlowNodeInstance
      Returns the ID of the process definition where this FlowNodeInstance is defined.
      Specified by:
      getProcessDefinitionId in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      the ID of the process definition.

    • getParentProcessInstanceId

      long getParentProcessInstanceId()
      Description copied from interface: FlowNodeInstance
      Always returns the directly containing process instance ID (at the lower level, if several levels of containing processes).
      Specified by:
      getParentProcessInstanceId in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      the ID of the lowest-level containing process instance.

    • getRootContainerId

      long getRootContainerId()
      Description copied from interface: FlowNodeInstance
      Returns the root container ID. It is the ID of the root-level containing process instance.
      Specified by:
      getRootContainerId in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      the root container ID.

    • getExecutedBy

      long getExecutedBy()
      Specified by:
      getExecutedBy in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      The identifier of the user who executed the flow node

    • getExecutedBySubstitute

      long getExecutedBySubstitute()
      Specified by:
      getExecutedBySubstitute in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      The identifier of the substitute user (as Process manager or Administrator) who executed the flow node.

    • getFlownodeDefinitionId

      long getFlownodeDefinitionId()
      Description copied from interface: FlowNodeInstance
      Returns the ID of the flow node definition of this instance.
      Specified by:
      getFlownodeDefinitionId in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      the ID of the flow node definition that this FlowNodeInstance is an instance of.

      Date getClaimedDate()

    • getStateCategory

      StateCategory getStateCategory()
      Not exposed in JSON — the HumanTaskItem REST shape does not carry this field. Still available through the Java API for callers that need the flow-node state category.
      Specified by:
      getStateCategory in interface FlowNodeInstance

    • getParentContainerId

      long getParentContainerId()
      Not exposed in JSON — the HumanTaskItem REST shape does not carry this field. parentContainerId is the id of the immediate container the flow node lives inside; for a top-level human task it equals parentCaseId (the parent process instance), but for a sub-task attached to a parent user task it points at that user task instead (see BPMInstancesCreator). Hiding it avoids exposing a value whose semantics shift between cases — callers needing the parent-task-vs-parent-case distinction can use the Java API.
      Specified by:
      getParentContainerId in interface FlowNodeInstance
      Returns:
      the ID of the direct containing element (activity instance of process instance).
